sn4.1:1.2: thatили on one occasion, when the Blessed One was newly self-awakened, he was dwelling near Uruvelā on the bank of the Nerañjarā River, at the root of the Goatherd's Banyan Tree.
sn4.1:1.3: Then, when the Blessed One was alone in seclusion, this train of thought arose in his awareness,
sn4.1:1.4: “Released am I from that practice of extreme austerities!
sn4.1:1.5: How good that I’m released from that useless practice of extreme austerities!
sn4.1:1.6: How good that I’m released, that I have attained awakening!”
sn4.1:2.1: Then Māra the Evil One, having known with his awareness the train of thought in the Blessed One's awareness, went to the Blessed One and addressed him in verse:
sn4.1:3.2: by which young brahmans are purified,
sn4.1:3.4: You’ve missed the path to purity.”
sn4.1:4.1: Then, having understood that “This is Mara the Evil One,” the Blessed One replied to him in verses:
sn4.1:5.4: like oars & rudder on dry land:
sn4.1:6.4: But you, End-maker, are struck down.”
sn4.1:7.1: Then Mara the Evil One—sad & dejected at realizing, “The Blessed One knows me, the One Well-Gone knows me”—vanished right there.
